goal_id4d.config.title: Why identification matters for global development
goal_id4d.config.citation: Mahler, D G and Lambrechts, M. 2026. “Past, present and future: 100 years of challenges in tackling extreme poverty” In Atlas of Global Development 2026, edited by A F Pirlea, U Serajuddin, A Thudt, D Wadhwa and M Welch. Washington, DC: World Bank. License: Creative Commons Attribution CC BY 3.0 IGO. https://doi.org/10.60616/f7gk-me57
goal_id4d.content.#1.text: IDs are essential for participating in modern life.
goal_id4d.content.#4.title: Types of credentials
goal_id4d.content.#5.title: More than
goal_id4d.content.#5.units: people worldwide lack official proof of identity
goal_id4d.content.#6.text: Identification gaps continue to constrain development globally. Roughly 1 in 10 people globally remain without legal identification. Children account for over half of those without ID due to unregistered births.
goal_id4d.content.#7.title: Adults with ID by region
goal_id4d.content.#8.title: Foundational coverage
goal_id4d.content.#9.title: Population with and without ID
goal_id4d.content.#10.text: The strongest indicators of lacking Id are low education and being out of the work force.
goal_id4d.content.#11.title: Indicators of lacking ID
goal_id4d.content.#12.text: The gender gap remains above 5 percentage points in 9 countries.
goal_id4d.content.#13.title: Gender gap
goal_id4d.content.#16.title: Barriers of lacking ID
goal_id4d.content.#17.text: Barriers experienced for not having ID.
goal_id4d.content.#18.title: Barriers experienced
goal_id4d.content.#20.text: Why are they better? Secure remote authentication, fully digital services delivery, faster processing and service times
goal_id4d.content.#21.units: people lack online digital ID access
goal_id4d.content.#22.text: 88 countries now have advanced digital IDs.
goal_id4d.content.#23.title: Digital ID issued
goal_id4d.content.#24.text: Ownership ranges from 5% (Bolivia) to 81% (Türkiye). Usage rates vary widely, even where access exists and ownership is high (ex: Japan).
goal_id4d.content.#25.title: Digital ID ownership
goal_id4d.content.#26.title: Digital ID usage
goal_id4d.content.#27.text: Acces to digital ID does not guarantee use. Digital ID adoption is often limited by lack of relevant use cases, poor design and user friendliness, low digital literacy and limited access to devices.
goal_id4d.content.#28.title: Digital ID ownership and usage
goal_id4d.content.#29.text: Singapore of other country use case
goal_id4d.references.foster2025: Foster, Elizabeth ⓡ Jolliffe, D ⓡ Lara Ibarra, G ⓡ Lakner, C ⓡ Tetteh Baah, S. 2025. Global Poverty Revisited Using 2021 PPPs and New Data on Consumption. World Bank .
